architecture.summary: This is a survey paper on chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning in large language models. It does not present a novel system or mechanism, but rather systematically reviews existing methods across three dimensions: prompt construction (manual, automatic, semi-automatic), topological variants (chain, tree, graph structures), and enhancement methods (verification, decomposition, knowledge integration, ensembling, efficiency). The paper catalogs how information flows through LLM reasoning via step-by-step intermediate reasoning tokens and various architectural interventions without modifying model weights.

biblio.title: Navigate through Enigmatic Labyrinth A Survey of Chain of Thought Reasoning: Advances, Frontiers and Future

claims.statement: Chain-of-thought prompting significantly enhances LLM's reasoning capabilities
Chain-of-thought reasoning breaks down complex problems into manageable steps and establishes connections among these steps, thereby facilitating reasoning
Manual XoT demonstrates better performance than automatic prompting, but annotation of rationales incurs significant cost and introduces dilemmas in demonstration selection

Tree-based methods gain the capability to widely explore and backtrack during reasoning, and deliver preliminary global planning capabilities towards the global optimum, but are restricted by demands of explicit question decomposition and state transition, which leads to limitations in task generalization

Graph structures outperform tree-based methods in handling complex problems through improved modeling of sub-problem aggregation and self-verification, but rely on specially designed state decomposition, leading to poorer generalization
LLMs may not address issues beyond their own capabilities, which raises doubt on the effectiveness of self-feedback

Self-ensemble is an effective way to improve performance, but alongside the improvement in performance, there has been a multiplied increase in inference costs, which in turn limits its wide application
Distilling task-specific reasoning ability may adversely downgrade the general performance
